content: Cost-effectiveness is determined by the Incremental Cost-Effectiveness Ratio (ICER): (Cost_new - Cost_old) / (QALY_new - QALY_old). An ICER below the threshold indicates good value. QALYs are calculated by multiplying time spent in a health state by its utility weight (0=death, 1=perfect health), derived from instruments like EQ-5D or SF-6D. Criticisms of QALYs include: discrimination against disabled people (lower baseline utility), ageism (older people have fewer life-years to gain), and difficulty capturing caregiver burden. Alternative metrics include Disability-Adjusted Life Years (DALYs) used in global health, and Equal Value of Life Years Gained (evLYG) proposed in the US to avoid QALY limitations.
